title: papaver alpinum var. aurantiacum
intro: Papaver alpinum var. aurantiacum, also known as the alpine poppy, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Papaver alpinum var. aurantiacum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity and side effects.
